summary.title: Tanstack Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Threat Model

data.name: CI/CD pipeline
data.description: Automated assembly line that runs whenever a developer submits new code

data.name: TeamPCP
data.description: The name given to the hacker group

data.name: Malicious Fork
data.description: The fake TanStack code copy that has the malicious malware inside

data.name:
Payload
(Exec. on Installation)
data.description: The malicious payload that runs to get credentials

data.name: C2 Server
data.description: The Command and Control server that the attacker uses to receive the credentials

data.name: Workflow
data.description: Where the TanStack misconfiguration of pull_request_target happened/lived.

data.name: Build Cache
data.description: The shared build cache area is used to save time in setting up the tools and files needed to download but that got poisoned so every time it was now called it picked up poisoned files.

data.name: OIDC Publisher
data.description: Where the key to publishing npm packages were held and got used by attackers to poison more npm packages

data.name: Runner Memory
data.description: Where the secrets are hidden due to GitHub's action (RAM)

data.name: npm registry
data.description: Where are the infected npm packages ended up

data.name:
npm install
(triggers payload)
data.description: the command where the malware happened

data.name: Local Credentials
data.description: Where all the secrets were kept
